scope.closed_by_this_contract: The fixed B14 curved-host preview/commit minimum-radius mismatch is reproducible, quantified and protected against silent reinterpretation.
scope.still_open: The successor shared analytical preflight, preview/commit alignment, pre-mutation rejection test and wider crossover matrix are not implemented.
scope.product_effect: Evidence and acceptance rules only. Neither B14 nor B15 product behaviour is changed or approved as successor behaviour.
scope.legacy_defect_rule: The current mismatch is an immutable-oracle observation, not an invariant to reproduce in the Workbench.

b15.role: accepted behavioural reference with matching crossover functions

fixture.safety_rule: Open read-only in memory, perform no creation or save, close it, and require the source bytes and nine-object snapshot to remain unchanged.

request.complete_rule: minimum(turnout_a_mapped_road, turnout_b_mapped_road, connector) >= requested minimum radius

legacy_path_characterisation.preview: CrossoverManagerPanel.preview_geometry calls solve_rea_c10_crossover_geometry and caches its result. The solver validates the requested connector radius but does not calculate either mapped turnout-road minimum radius.
legacy_path_characterisation.commit: Crossover creation reuses the cached solver result. _build_rea_c10_crossover_geometry builds both exact turnout modules and connector shapes before taking the minimum of the two built turnout configuration radii and connector radius.
legacy_path_characterisation.consequence: A connector-feasible placement can pass preview, spend exact-shape construction time and only then fail the complete gate. The document remains transactionally recoverable, but the preview is not authoritative and avoidable work occurs.
legacy_path_characterisation.available_pure_boundary: turnout_mapping_metrics already returns the mapped turnout-road minimum radius from host station data without creating a document object or Part shape.

shared_result: persisted Host A and Host B identities
all request values and a deterministic input signature
resolved Host B chainage, orientations and handing
accepted or rejected decision with a component-specific diagnostic
rules: Preview and create/edit/extend must call or reuse one shared analytical preflight implementing the declared complete rule.
A reused preflight must be rejected if either persisted host identity, geometry signature or any request input changed.
The 500.000 mm witness must be rejected before a Part shape, document object, transaction, history entry, recompute or persisted metadata change is created.
The 746.298 mm witness must remain accepted and later exact construction must agree with every preflight component radius within the declared tolerance.
Failure must name the limiting component, actual radius and configured minimum; diagnostics must be identical at preview and commit boundaries.
The future regression must exercise create, edit and turnout-extension entry points plus invalidation after every signature input class changes.
successor_acceptance.migration_gate: Do not move or remove the B14 crossover path until the successor regression proves both witnesses, zero mutation on rejection, exact-build agreement on acceptance and user-approved GUI evidence.
